The watch comes with its original box, papers, booklets, tag and more, and was originally sold in Hong Kong. #31 - The Rolex Explorer II "Polar" 16570 w/ Original Box & Papers Set 2001 The History— The Example The Rolex Explorer II evolved from the earlier Explorer models by introducing a fixed 24-hour bezel and additional GMT hand, transforming it from a simple time-only tool watch into a purpose-built companion for explorers working in environments where distinguishing day from night could prove difficult. The reference 16570 is widely regarded as the sweet spot of the Explorer II lineage, combining classic proportions with the reliable Rolex calibre 3185 movement and independently adjustable GMT hand. Dating to 2001 , this “Polar” example features the model’s iconic white dial, offering superb legibility and a distinctive look that has become increasingly popular with collectors. Presented in excellent, sharp condition , the watch has been exceptionally well preserved and is accompanied by its original box, papers, booklets, tag and accessories . Originally supplied in Hong Kong , it is a superb, complete example of one of Rolex’s most wearable and increasingly collectible sports watches.
